logarithmicScales.name: Decibels
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a logarithmic level in decibels (dB).
logarithmicScales.remarks: Decibels express ratios on a logarithmic scale. Amplitude/field quantities use dB = 20·log10(ratio); power quantities use dB = 10·log10(ratio). A level of 0 dB is unity.

conversions.fromSummary: Creates a level from an amplitude gain using dB = 20·log10(gain).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this level to a linear amplitude gain using gain = 10^(dB/20).

conversions.fromSummary: Creates a level from a linear power ratio using dB = 10·log10(ratio).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this level to a linear power ratio using ratio = 10^(dB/10).

logarithmicScales.name: Cents
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a musical pitch interval in cents (1/100 of an equal-tempered semitone).

conversions.fromSummary: Creates an interval from a frequency ratio using cents = 1200·log2(ratio).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this interval to a frequency ratio using ratio = 2^(cents/1200).

logarithmicScales.name: Semitones
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a musical pitch interval in equal-tempered semitones.

conversions.fromSummary: Creates an interval from a frequency ratio using semitones = 12·log2(ratio).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this interval to a frequency ratio using ratio = 2^(semitones/12).

logarithmicScales.name: SoundPressureLevel
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a sound pressure level (SPL) in decibels relative to the 20 µPa threshold of hearing.
logarithmicScales.remarks: SPL is a logarithmic field quantity: SPL = 20·log10(p / p₀) with p₀ = 20 µPa.

conversions.fromSummary: Creates a level from a linear sound pressure using SPL = 20·log10(p / p₀).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this level to the equivalent linear sound pressure using p = p₀·10^(SPL/20).

logarithmicScales.name: SoundIntensityLevel
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a sound intensity level (SIL) in decibels relative to the 10⁻¹² W/m² threshold of hearing.
logarithmicScales.remarks: SIL is a logarithmic power quantity: SIL = 10·log10(I / I₀) with I₀ = 10⁻¹² W/m².

conversions.fromSummary: Creates a level from a linear sound intensity using SIL = 10·log10(I / I₀).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this level to the equivalent linear sound intensity using I = I₀·10^(SIL/10).

logarithmicScales.name: SoundPowerLevel
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a sound power level (SWL) in decibels relative to the 10⁻¹² W reference sound power.
logarithmicScales.remarks: SWL is a logarithmic power quantity: SWL = 10·log10(P / P₀) with P₀ = 10⁻¹² W.

conversions.fromSummary: Creates a level from a linear sound power using SWL = 10·log10(P / P₀).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this level to the equivalent linear sound power using P = P₀·10^(SWL/10).

logarithmicScales.name: DirectionalityIndex
logarithmicScales.description: Represents a directivity index (DI) in decibels — how much more intense a source or receiver is on-axis than its spherical average.

conversions.fromSummary: Creates an index from the linear on-axis-to-average intensity ratio using DI = 10·log10(ratio).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this index to the linear intensity ratio using ratio = 10^(DI/10).

logarithmicScales.name: PH
logarithmicScales.description: Represents acidity on the pH scale: pH = −log10([H⁺]) with the hydrogen-ion activity in mol/L.
logarithmicScales.remarks: pH is a logarithmic scale over the linear Concentration quantity (stored in the SI base mol/m³; 1 mol/L = 1000 mol/m³).

conversions.fromSummary: Creates a pH from a hydrogen-ion concentration using pH = −log10([H⁺] in mol/L).
conversions.toSummary: Converts this pH to the equivalent hydrogen-ion concentration using [H⁺] = 10^(−pH) mol/L.
